contents.value:
```r
if (condition) expr
if (condition) expr1 else expr2
```
Conditional execution. Evaluates `expr` (or `expr1`/`expr2`) depending on the logical value of `condition`.

```r
if (condition) expr1 else expr2
```
Alternative branch of an `if` statement. Must appear on the same line as the closing `}` of the if-block.

```r
for (var in seq) expr
```
Iterate over the elements of a vector or list. `var` takes each value in `seq` in turn.

```r
while (condition) expr
```
Repeatedly evaluate `expr` as long as `condition` is `TRUE`.

```r
repeat expr
```
Infinite loop. Must use `break` to exit.

```r
for (var in seq) ...
```
Used in `for` loops to specify the sequence to iterate over.

```r
next
```
Skip the remainder of the current loop iteration and proceed to the next iteration.

```r
break
```
Exit the innermost `for`, `while`, or `repeat` loop immediately.

```r
function(arglist) body
```
Create a function object. The last evaluated expression is the return value. Use `return()` for early exit.

```r
return(value)
```
Explicitly return a value from a function and exit the function body.

```r
TRUE
```
Logical constant for truth. `T` is an abbreviation but can be overwritten; `TRUE` cannot.

```r
FALSE
```
Logical constant for false. `F` is an abbreviation but can be overwritten; `FALSE` cannot.

```r
NULL
```
The null object representing the absence of a value. Has length 0 and no type.

```r
NA
```
Logical constant representing a missing value. Propagates through most operations. Use `is.na()` to test.

```r
NA_integer_
```
Missing value constant of type integer. Useful when explicit type is needed.

```r
NA_real_
```
Missing value constant of type double.

```r
NA_complex_
```
Missing value constant of type complex.

```r
NA_character_
```
Missing value constant of type character.

```r
Inf
```
Positive infinity. Result of `1/0`. Use `is.infinite()` to test.

```r
NaN
```
Not a Number. Result of `0/0`. Use `is.nan()` to test. `is.na(NaN)` is also `TRUE`.

```r
library(package, help, pos = 2, lib.loc = NULL, character.only = FALSE, logical.return = FALSE, warn.conflicts = TRUE, quietly = FALSE, verbose = getOption("verbose"))
```
Load and attach an add-on package. Stops with an error if the package is not found.

```r
require(package, lib.loc = NULL, quietly = FALSE, warn.conflicts = TRUE, character.only = FALSE) -> logical
```
Try to load and attach a package. Returns `TRUE` if successful, `FALSE` otherwise (unlike `library` which errors).

```r
source(file, local = FALSE, echo = verbose, print.eval = echo, verbose = getOption("verbose"), encoding = "unknown")
```
Read R expressions from a file and evaluate them in the specified environment.

```r
local(expr, envir = new.env())
```
Evaluate an expression in a temporary local environment. Variables created inside do not persist.

```r
switch(EXPR, ...)
```
Select one of several alternatives. If `EXPR` is character, matches named arguments. If numeric, selects by position.

```r
print(x, ...)
```
Generic function for printing its argument. Methods dispatch on the class of `x`.

```r
cat(..., file = "", sep = " ", fill = FALSE, labels = NULL, append = FALSE)
```
Concatenate and print. Outputs the objects, concatenating with `sep`. Does not add a newline by default.

```r
paste(..., sep = " ", collapse = NULL) -> character
```
Concatenate strings. `sep` separates elements pairwise; `collapse` collapses the resulting vector into one string.

```r
paste0(..., collapse = NULL) -> character
```
Equivalent to `paste(..., sep = "")`. Concatenates with no separator.

```r
sprintf(fmt, ...) -> character
```
C-style formatted string. Supports `%s`, `%d`, `%f`, `%e`, `%g`, `%x`, `%%`, and width/precision modifiers.

```r
c(..., recursive = FALSE) -> vector
```
Combine values into a vector or list. All arguments are coerced to a common type (hierarchy: NULL < raw < logical < integer < double < complex < character < list < expression).

```r
length(x) -> integer
length(x) <- value
```
Get or set the length of vectors, lists, and other objects.

```r
nchar(x, type = "chars", allowNA = FALSE, keepNA = NA) -> integer
```
Return the number of characters (or bytes/width) in each element of a character vector.

```r
seq(from, to, by, length.out, along.with) -> vector
```
Generate regular sequences. Arguments: starting value, end value, step size, desired length, or template object.

```r
seq_along(along.with) -> integer
```
Generate a sequence `1, 2, ..., length(along.with)`. Faster and safer than `1:length(x)` when `x` may be empty.

```r
seq_len(length.out) -> integer
```
Generate the sequence `1, 2, ..., length.out`. Safer than `1:n` when `n` may be 0.

```r
rep(x, times = 1, length.out = NA, each = 1) -> vector
```
Replicate elements of a vector. `times` replicates the whole vector; `each` replicates each element.

```r
rev(x) -> vector
```
Reverse the order of elements in a vector or list.

```r
sort(x, decreasing = FALSE, na.last = NA, ...) -> vector
```
Sort a vector. By default ascending; set `decreasing = TRUE` for descending. NAs are removed by default.

```r
order(..., na.last = TRUE, decreasing = FALSE, method = c("auto", "shell", "radix")) -> integer
```
Return a permutation that rearranges its first argument into ascending or descending order.

```r
which(x, arr.ind = FALSE, useNames = TRUE) -> integer
```
Return the indices of elements that are `TRUE`. `which.min()` / `which.max()` return the index of the extremum.

```r
match(x, table, nomatch = NA_integer_, incomparables = NULL) -> integer
```
Return the positions of first matches of `x` in `table`. The `%in%` operator uses `match` internally.

```r
unique(x, incomparables = FALSE, fromLast = FALSE, ...) -> vector
```
Return a vector with duplicate elements removed. Preserves original order.

```r
duplicated(x, incomparables = FALSE, fromLast = FALSE, ...) -> logical
```
Return a logical vector indicating which elements are duplicates of elements with smaller subscripts.

```r
table(..., exclude = if (useNA == "no") c(NA, NaN), useNA = c("no", "ifany", "always"), dnn, deparse.level = 1) -> table
```
Build a contingency table of factor level counts.

```r
sum(..., na.rm = FALSE) -> numeric
```
Return the sum of all values in its arguments. Set `na.rm = TRUE` to remove NA values.

```r
mean(x, trim = 0, na.rm = FALSE, ...) -> numeric
```
Compute the arithmetic mean. `trim` specifies the fraction (0 to 0.5) of observations to trim from each end.

```r
median(x, na.rm = FALSE) -> numeric
```
Compute the sample median.

```r
sd(x, na.rm = FALSE) -> numeric
```
Compute the standard deviation (uses `n-1` denominator).

```r
var(x, y = NULL, na.rm = FALSE, use) -> numeric|matrix
```
Compute the variance of `x`, or the covariance between `x` and `y`.

```r
min(..., na.rm = FALSE) -> numeric
```
Return the minimum of all input values.

```r
max(..., na.rm = FALSE) -> numeric
```
Return the maximum of all input values.

```r
range(..., na.rm = FALSE, finite = FALSE) -> numeric(2)
```
Return a vector containing the minimum and maximum of all the given arguments.

```r
abs(x) -> numeric
```
Compute the absolute (non-negative) value.

```r
sqrt(x) -> numeric
```
Compute the square root. Returns `NaN` for negative values (use `sqrt(as.complex(x))` for complex result).

```r
log(x, base = exp(1)) -> numeric
```
Compute the logarithm. Default is natural log; specify `base` for others.

```r
log2(x) -> numeric
```
Compute the base-2 logarithm.

```r
log10(x) -> numeric
```
Compute the base-10 (common) logarithm.

```r
exp(x) -> numeric
```
Compute the exponential function e^x.

```r
ceiling(x) -> numeric
```
Round up to the smallest integer not less than `x`.

```r
floor(x) -> numeric
```
Round down to the largest integer not greater than `x`.

```r
round(x, digits = 0) -> numeric
```
Round to the specified number of decimal places. Uses IEC 60559 (banker's) rounding.

```r
trunc(x, ...) -> numeric
```
Truncate towards zero (round towards zero).

```r
sign(x) -> numeric
```
Return the signs of the elements: -1 for negative, 0 for zero, 1 for positive.

```r
cumsum(x) -> numeric
```
Return a vector of cumulative sums.

```r
cumprod(x) -> numeric
```
Return a vector of cumulative products.

```r
cummax(x) -> numeric
```
Return a vector of cumulative maxima.

```r
cummin(x) -> numeric
```
Return a vector of cumulative minima.

```r
diff(x, lag = 1, differences = 1, ...) -> numeric
```
Return lagged differences. `differences` controls how many times to apply the differencing.

```r
head(x, n = 6L) -> object
```
Return the first `n` elements or rows. Negative `n` returns all but the last `|n|` elements.

```r
tail(x, n = 6L) -> object
```
Return the last `n` elements or rows.

```r
nrow(x) -> integer|NULL
```
Return the number of rows of a matrix, array, or data frame. Returns `NULL` for vectors.

```r
ncol(x) -> integer|NULL
```
Return the number of columns of a matrix, array, or data frame.

```r
dim(x) -> integer|NULL
dim(x) <- value
```
Get or set the dimension attribute. For a matrix, returns c(nrow, ncol).

```r
names(x) -> character|NULL
names(x) <- value
```
Get or set the names attribute of an object.

```r
colnames(x, do.NULL = TRUE, prefix = "col") -> character|NULL
colnames(x) <- value
```
Get or set column names of a matrix-like object.

```r
rownames(x, do.NULL = TRUE, prefix = "row") -> character|NULL
rownames(x) <- value
```
Get or set row names of a matrix-like object.

```r
str(object, max.level = NA, vec.len = 4, ...) -> invisible(NULL)
```
Compactly display the internal structure of an R object. A diagnostic function.

```r
summary(object, ...) -> varies
```
Generic function for producing result summaries. Output depends on the class of the object.

```r
class(x) -> character
class(x) <- value
```
Get or set the class attribute. Determines method dispatch for S3 generic functions.

```r
typeof(x) -> character
```
Return the internal type/storage mode: "logical", "integer", "double", "complex", "character", "list", etc.

```r
is.numeric(x) -> logical
```
Return `TRUE` if `x` is of numeric type (integer or double) and not a factor.

```r
is.character(x) -> logical
```
Return `TRUE` if `x` is of type character.

```r
is.logical(x) -> logical
```
Return `TRUE` if `x` is of type logical.

```r
is.na(x) -> logical
```
Return a logical vector indicating which elements are `NA` or `NaN`.

```r
is.null(x) -> logical
```
Return `TRUE` if `x` is `NULL`.

```r
as.numeric(x, ...) -> double
```
Coerce to double-precision numeric. Identical to `as.double()`.

```r
as.character(x, ...) -> character
```
Coerce to character (string) representation.

```r
as.logical(x, ...) -> logical
```
Coerce to logical. Numbers: 0 → FALSE, non-zero → TRUE. Strings: "TRUE"/"FALSE".

```r
as.integer(x, ...) -> integer
```
Coerce to integer. Truncates doubles towards zero.

```r
as.double(x, ...) -> double
```
Coerce to double-precision floating point number.

```r
as.data.frame(x, row.names = NULL, optional = FALSE, ...) -> data.frame
```
Coerce to data frame. Handles matrices, lists, vectors, and other objects.

```r
data.frame(..., row.names = NULL, check.rows = FALSE, check.names = TRUE, fix.empty.names = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE) -> data.frame
```
Create a data frame from the supplied column vectors or lists.

```r
matrix(data = NA, nrow = 1, ncol = 1, byrow = FALSE, dimnames = NULL) -> matrix
```
Create a matrix from the given data, filled column-wise by default.

```r
array(data = NA, dim = length(data), dimnames = NULL) -> array
```
Create a multi-dimensional array.

```r
list(...) -> list
```
Construct a list. Lists can contain elements of different types and are accessed with `[[` or `$`.

```r
vector(mode = "logical", length = 0L) -> vector
```
Create a vector of the specified mode and length, initialized to default values.

```r
factor(x, levels, labels = levels, exclude = NA, ordered = is.ordered(x)) -> factor
```
Encode a vector as a factor. Levels define the set of allowed values.

```r
read.csv(file, header = TRUE, sep = ",", quote = "\"", dec = ".", fill = TRUE, comment.char = "", ...) -> data.frame
```
Read a comma-separated values file into a data frame.

```r
read.table(file, header = FALSE, sep = "", quote = "\"'", dec = ".", ...) -> data.frame
```
Read a file in table format into a data frame.

```r
write.csv(x, file = "", row.names = TRUE, ...) -> invisible(NULL)
```
Write a data frame to a comma-separated values file.

```r
write.table(x, file = "", append = FALSE, quote = TRUE, sep = " ", eol = "\n", na = "NA", dec = ".", row.names = TRUE, col.names = TRUE) -> invisible(NULL)
```
Write a data frame or matrix to a file.

```r
readRDS(file, refhook = NULL) -> object
```
Read a single R object from a file serialized with `saveRDS()`.

```r
saveRDS(object, file = "", ascii = FALSE, version = NULL, compress = TRUE, refhook = NULL)
```
Serialize a single R object to a file. Use `readRDS()` to restore.

```r
file.path(..., fsep = .Platform$file.sep) -> character
```
Construct file paths from components in a platform-independent way.

```r
file.exists(...) -> logical
```
Return `TRUE` for each argument that is an existing file (not directory, by default).

```r
dir.create(path, showWarnings = TRUE, recursive = FALSE, mode = "0777")
```
Create a directory. With `recursive = TRUE`, create parent directories as needed.

```r
getwd() -> character
```
Return the absolute path of the current working directory.

```r
setwd(dir) -> character
```
Set the working directory. Returns the previous working directory (invisibly).

```r
Sys.time() -> POSIXct
```
Return the current date and time as a POSIXct object.

```r
Sys.sleep(time)
```
Suspend execution for `time` seconds (can be fractional).

```r
system(command, intern = FALSE, ignore.stdout = FALSE, ignore.stderr = FALSE, wait = TRUE, input = NULL, ...) -> integer|character
```
Invoke an OS command. With `intern = TRUE`, captures stdout as a character vector.

```r
system2(command, args = character(), stdout = "", stderr = "", stdin = "", input = NULL, env = character(), wait = TRUE, ...) -> integer|character
```
Interface to OS commands. Safer than `system()` — command and args are separate.

```r
tryCatch(expr, ..., finally)
```
Evaluate `expr` with condition handlers. Handlers are named arguments matching condition classes (e.g., `error`, `warning`, `message`). `finally` is always evaluated.

```r
withCallingHandlers(expr, ...)
```
Establish calling handlers. Unlike `tryCatch`, execution continues in the calling context after the handler returns.

```r
stop(..., call. = TRUE, domain = NULL)
```
Stop execution and signal an error condition with the given message.

```r
warning(..., call. = TRUE, immediate. = FALSE, noBreaks. = FALSE, domain = NULL)
```
Generate a warning message.

```r
message(..., domain = NULL, appendLF = TRUE)
```
Generate a diagnostic message to stderr. Can be suppressed with `suppressMessages()`.

```r
format(x, ...) -> character
```
Format an R object for pretty printing. Many classes have specific methods.

```r
substr(x, start, stop) -> character
substr(x, start, stop) <- value
```
Extract or replace substrings in character vectors.

```r
substring(text, first, last = 1000000L) -> character
```
Extract substrings. Compatible with replacement and vectorized over all arguments.

```r
gsub(pattern, replacement, x, ignore.case = FALSE, perl = FALSE, fixed = FALSE, useBytes = FALSE) -> character
```
Replace all occurrences of `pattern` in `x` with `replacement`.

```r
sub(pattern, replacement, x, ignore.case = FALSE, perl = FALSE, fixed = FALSE, useBytes = FALSE) -> character
```
Replace the first occurrence of `pattern` in `x` with `replacement`.

```r
grep(pattern, x, ignore.case = FALSE, perl = FALSE, value = FALSE, fixed = FALSE, useBytes = FALSE, invert = FALSE) -> integer|character
```
Search for matches to `pattern`. Returns indices by default, or matching values if `value = TRUE`.

```r
grepl(pattern, x, ignore.case = FALSE, perl = FALSE, fixed = FALSE, useBytes = FALSE) -> logical
```
Return a logical vector indicating which elements of `x` match `pattern`.

```r
regexpr(pattern, text, ignore.case = FALSE, perl = FALSE, fixed = FALSE, useBytes = FALSE) -> integer
```
Find the first match of `pattern` in each element of `text`. Returns starting position with `match.length` attribute.

```r
gregexpr(pattern, text, ignore.case = FALSE, perl = FALSE, fixed = FALSE, useBytes = FALSE) -> list
```
Find all matches of `pattern` in each element of `text`.

```r
strsplit(x, split, fixed = FALSE, perl = FALSE, useBytes = FALSE) -> list
```
Split elements of a character vector by a pattern. Returns a list of character vectors.

```r
trimws(x, which = c("both", "left", "right"), whitespace = "[ \t\r\n]") -> character
```
Remove leading and/or trailing whitespace from character strings.

```r
toupper(x) -> character
```
Convert all characters to upper case.

```r
tolower(x) -> character
```
Convert all characters to lower case.

```r
startsWith(x, prefix) -> logical
```
Test whether elements of `x` start with `prefix`.

```r
endsWith(x, suffix) -> logical
```
Test whether elements of `x` end with `suffix`.

```r
apply(X, MARGIN, FUN, ..., simplify = TRUE) -> array|list
```
Apply a function over array margins. `MARGIN = 1` for rows, `2` for columns.

```r
sapply(X, FUN, ..., simplify = TRUE, USE.NAMES = TRUE) -> vector|matrix|list
```
Apply a function and simplify the result to a vector or matrix if possible.

```r
lapply(X, FUN, ...) -> list
```
Apply a function over a list or vector and return a list.

```r
vapply(X, FUN, FUN.VALUE, ..., USE.NAMES = TRUE) -> array
```
Apply a function with a pre-specified return type. Safer than `sapply` because the return type is checked.

```r
tapply(X, INDEX, FUN = NULL, ..., default = NA, simplify = TRUE) -> array
```
Apply a function to groups of values defined by a factor or list of factors.

```r
mapply(FUN, ..., MoreArgs = NULL, SIMPLIFY = TRUE, USE.NAMES = TRUE) -> list|vector
```
Multivariate version of `sapply`. Applies `FUN` to the corresponding elements of each argument.

```r
Map(f, ...) -> list
```
Apply `f` to corresponding elements. Wrapper for `mapply(..., SIMPLIFY = FALSE)`.

```r
Reduce(f, x, init, right = FALSE, accumulate = FALSE) -> object
```
Reduce a list to a single value by applying a binary function cumulatively.

```r
Filter(f, x) -> list|vector
```
Extract elements of `x` for which `f(element)` is `TRUE`.

```r
do.call(what, args, quote = FALSE, envir = parent.frame()) -> object
```
Execute a function call with arguments supplied as a list.

```r
Vectorize(FUN, vectorize.args = arg.names, SIMPLIFY = TRUE, USE.NAMES = TRUE) -> function
```
Create a vectorized version of a function that is not already vectorized.

```r
ifelse(test, yes, no) -> vector
```
Vectorized conditional. Returns `yes` where `test` is TRUE, `no` where FALSE.

```r
plot(x, y, ...) -> NULL
```
Generic X-Y plotting function. Dispatches on the class of the first argument.

```r
hist(x, breaks = "Sturges", freq = NULL, col = NULL, main = paste("Histogram of", xname), ...) -> histogram
```
Compute and plot a histogram of the given data.

```r
barplot(height, ..., width = 1, space = NULL, names.arg = NULL, col = NULL) -> numeric
```
Create a bar plot with vertical or horizontal bars.

```r
boxplot(formula, data = NULL, ..., range = 1.5, col = NULL) -> list
```
Produce box-and-whisker plot(s) of the given (grouped) values.

```r
ggplot(data = NULL, mapping = aes(), ..., environment = parent.frame()) -> ggplot
```
Initialize a ggplot object. Use `+` to add layers, scales, and themes.

```r
aes(x, y, ...) -> mapping
```
Construct aesthetic mappings for ggplot2 layers. Maps data variables to visual properties.

```r
install.packages(pkgs, lib, repos = getOption("repos"), ...) -> invisible(NULL)
```
Download and install packages from CRAN or other repositories.

```r
lm(formula, data, subset, weights, na.action, method = "qr", model = TRUE, ...) -> lm
```
Fit a linear model using ordinary least squares.

```r
glm(formula, family = gaussian, data, weights, subset, na.action, ...) -> glm
```
Fit a generalized linear model with the specified family (binomial, poisson, etc.).

```r
t.test(x, y = NULL, alternative = c("two.sided", "less", "greater"), mu = 0, paired = FALSE, var.equal = FALSE, conf.level = 0.95) -> htest
```
Perform one-sample or two-sample Student's t-test.

```r
cor(x, y = NULL, use = "everything", method = c("pearson", "kendall", "spearman")) -> numeric|matrix
```
Compute correlation coefficients between vectors or columns of a matrix.

```r
predict(object, newdata, ...) -> varies
```
Generic function for predictions from model fits. Output depends on model class.

```r
setClass(Class, representation, prototype, contains, validity, sealed, ...) -> Class
```
Define an S4 class with typed slots and optional inheritance.

```r
setGeneric(name, def, ...) -> name
```
Define an S4 generic function for method dispatch.

```r
setMethod(f, signature, definition, ...) -> f
```
Define an S4 method for a generic function and signature.

```r
R6Class(classname, public, private, active, inherit, lock_objects, class, portable, lock_class, cloneable, parent_env) -> R6ClassGenerator
```
Create an R6 class generator (requires R6 package). Supports public/private fields, active bindings, and inheritance.

```r
new.env(hash = TRUE, parent = parent.frame(), size = 29L) -> environment
```
Create a new environment with the specified parent.

```r
environment(fun = NULL) -> environment
environment(fun) <- value
```
Get or set the environment of a function. `environment()` with no args returns the current environment.

```r
exists(x, where = -1, envir, frame, mode = "any", inherits = TRUE) -> logical
```
Return `TRUE` if a variable with the given name exists in the specified environment.

```r
get(x, envir = parent.frame(), mode = "any", inherits = TRUE) -> object
```
Search for an object by name and return it.

```r
assign(x, value, envir = parent.frame(), inherits = FALSE, immediate = TRUE)
```
Assign a value to a name in the specified environment.

```r
rm(..., list = character(), envir = parent.frame(), inherits = FALSE)
```
Remove objects from the specified environment.

```r
ls(name, envir, all.names = FALSE, pattern, sorted = TRUE) -> character
```
Return a character vector of the names of objects in the specified environment.

```r
na.rm = TRUE
```
Common parameter in many functions that removes NA values before computation.

```r
Sys.getenv(x = NULL, unset = '', names = NA)
```
Gets the values of environment variables.

```r
readLines(con, n = -1, ok = TRUE, warn = TRUE, encoding = 'unknown', skipNul = FALSE)
```
Read lines from a connection.

```r
writeLines(text, con = stdout(), sep = '\n', useBytes = FALSE)
```
Write text lines to a connection.
